commonCitations.medical_records_fees_hospital: Tex. Health & Safety Code § 241.154 (hospital records — fees set by Texas Board rule)
commonCitations.medical_records_fees_physician: 22 Tex. Admin. Code § 165.2 (TMB rule — reasonable, cost-based fees for physician records)
commonCitations.hospital_lien: Tex. Prop. Code §§ 55.001–55.008 (Hospital and Emergency Medical Services Liens)
commonCitations.minor_settlement_approval: Tex. Prop. Code § 142.001 et seq.; Tex. R. Civ. P. 173 (next friend; friendly suit or § 142 trust typically required)
commonCitations.tort_claims_act_deadline_days: 180 (state default: 6 months under § 101.101(a); home-rule municipalities may require shorter notice — e.g., 90 days in Houston/San Antonio)
commonCitations.contingency_fee_rule: Tex. Disciplinary R. Prof. Conduct 1.04 (no statutory percentage cap for PI; reasonableness standard)
commonCitations.certificate_of_merit_medmal: Tex. Civ. Prac. & Rem. Code § 74.351 (expert report required within 120 days of defendant's answer)
commonCitations.damages_cap_medmal_noneconomic: Tex. Civ. Prac. & Rem. Code § 74.301 ($250k per physician/provider; $500k cumulative against all non-physician facilities)
commonCitations.comparative_negligence_standard: Tex. Civ. Prac. & Rem. Code §§ 33.001–33.017 (modified comparative; plaintiff barred if >50% at fault)
commonCitations.threshold_serious_injury: N/A (Texas is an at-fault jurisdiction; no serious-injury threshold for PI claims)
